reasoning: Vaccines typicaly take years to develop and go through clinical trials. Current literature (such as papers published in June 2025) explicitly note that recent findings will "facilitate... vaccine development," indicating that a vaccine does not currently exist. A review of the Wikipedia page for "List of infectious diseases" confirms there is no vaccine listed for Human bocavirus as of October 2025. Given the extremely short forecast horizons (ranging from 1 week to 3 months), it is highly improbable that a vaccine will be developed, approved, and listed on the Wikipedia page by any of the resolution dates. The base rate for such vaccine development within a short timeframe is practically zero. Therefore, I assign the minimum allowable probability (0.02) to the near-term dates and slightly increase it for the furthest date to account for any unforeseen changes or anomalies.
